question_text: Which of the following is/are the eigen vector(s) for the matrix given below? $ \left( { \begin{array} { r r r r r } { - 9 } & { - 6 } & { - 2 } & { - 4 } \\ { - 8 } & { - 6 } & { - 3 } & { - 1 } \\ { 2 0 } & { 1 5 } & { 8 } & { 5 } \\ { 3 2 } & { 2 1 } & { 7 } & { 1 2 } \end{array} } \right)\tag{a} $ $ \begin{array} { r } { \left( \begin{array} { c } { - 1 } \\ { 1 } \\ { 0 } \\ { 1 } \end{array} \right) } \end{array} $
solution:
To determine which of the given vectors is an eigenvector of the matrix \( A = \left( \begin{array} { r r r r r } { - 9 } & { - 6 } & { - 2 } & { - 4 } \\ { - 8 } & { - 6 } & { - 3 } & { - 1 } \\ { 2 0 } & { 1 5 } & { 8 } & { 5 } \\ { 3 2 } & { 2 1 } & { 7 } & { 1 2 } \end{array} \right) \), we need to check if each vector satisfies the equation \( A \mathbf{v} = \lambda \mathbf{v} \) for some scalar \(\lambda\).

Let's check each option one by one.

**Option (A):** \( \mathbf{v} = \begin{pmatrix} -1 \\ 1 \\ 0 \\ 1 \end{pmatrix} \)

Calculate \( A \mathbf{v} \):
\[
A \mathbf{v} = \left( \begin{array} { r r r r r } { - 9 } & { - 6 } & { - 2 } & { - 4 } \\ { - 8 } & { - 6 } & { - 3 } & { - 1 } \\ { 2 0 } & { 1 5 } & { 8 } & { 5 } \\ { 3 2 } & { 2 1 } & { 7 } & { 1 2 } \end{array} \right) \begin{pmatrix} -1 \\ 1 \\ 0 \\ 1 \end{pmatrix} = \begin{pmatrix} -9(-1) + (-6)(1) + (-2)(0) + (-4)(1) \\ -8(-1) + (-6)(1) + (-3)(0) + (-1)(1) \\ 20(-1) + 15(1) + 8(0) + 5(1) \\ 32(-1) + 21(1) + 7(0) + 12(1) \end{pmatrix} = \begin{pmatrix} 9 - 6 + 0 - 4 \\ 8 - 6 + 0 - 1 \\ -20 + 15 + 0 + 5 \\ -32 + 21 + 0 + 12 \end{pmatrix} = \begin{pmatrix} -1 \\ 1 \\ 0 \\ 1 \end{pmatrix}
\]
Since \( A \mathbf{v} = \mathbf{v} \), \(\mathbf{v}\) is an eigenvector with eigenvalue \(\lambda = 1\).

A \mathbf{v} = \left( \begin
